name: hackrf
title: hackrf
description:
Software defined radio peripheral - utilities
HackRF is an open source Software Defined Radio that can receive
and transmit between 30 MHz and 6 GHz. HackRF has a 20 MHz bandwidth.
It is a High Speed USB device powered by the USB bus.
.
This package contains a set of command line utilities:
* hackrf_clock: HackRF clock configuration utility
* hackrf_cpldjtag: program CLPD
* hackrf_debug: chip register read/write/config tool
* hackrf_info: probe device and show configuration
* hackrf_operacake: control of operacake board via hackrf
* hackrf_spiflash: read and write flash data from file.
* hackrf_sweep: control frequency sweep of hackrf
* hackrf_transfer: file based transmit and receive sdr

commands.name: hackrf_operacake
commands.description: control of operacake board via hackrf

When the engagement is in the install phase, use hackrf_biast as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hackrf_biast -R : Resets biast setting to default.` as documented upstream for hackrf_biast.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair hackrf_biast with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `hackrf_biast --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
